title: fix `NapiHandleScopeImpl` race condition
category: N-API handle-scope data race (unsynchronized m_storage mutation across threads)
crash_site: Intermittent SIGSEGV / heap corruption observed during GC sweep or value finalization while a native addon is under load: a read of a stale slot or a WTF::Vector whose backing store was reallocated underneath the reader. The crash surfaces in correct GC/finalization code that READS m_storage, NOT in the unsynchronized append() that actually caused it.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: the GC-sweep / finalizer code that READS m_storage (where corruption surfaces but which is correct); NapiHandleScope open/close lifecycle; WTF::Vector growth itself (correct). NapiHandleScopeImpl::reserveSlot is a CO-CAUSE, not a decoy.
ground_truth.fix_summary: NapiHandleScopeImpl::append and NapiHandleScopeImpl::reserveSlot mutate m_storage (a WTF::Vector) with no lock, but they run from multiple threads (e.g. GC sweeping / finalizers), so a concurrent append triggers a Vector reallocation that races another thread's access -> use-after-free / corruption. Fix: take `WTF::Locker locker { cellLock() }` before the m_storage mutation in both append() and reserveSlot().

admission.note: monogram+haiku vs baseline+haiku discrimination. HARD: crash is intermittent GC/finalization corruption with no text bridge to the unsynchronized m_storage append; the live decoy is the correct read/crash site. Authored from PR #20093 fix diff: append() and reserveSlot() gained WTF::Locker{cellLock()} around the m_storage mutation. NOTE: symptom is inferred from the race mechanism (no verbatim upstream crash trace was available).

provenance: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/pull/20093 — fix `NapiHandleScopeImpl` race condition (merged 2025-05-31); fix commit 4534f6e635d2; base c62a7a77a3fe = merge first-parent. Fixes #16146. Root cause + fix extracted from the commit diff (append/reserveSlot gained WTF::Locker{cellLock()}).
